agent_summary: Treat coding-agent/heuristic learning as infrastructure around the neural chess engine: capture failures, write invariant tests, route teachers, mine hard positions, tune search schedules, and periodically distill those discoveries back into model training.

agent_summary: Tiny Leela may need Rust, TypeScript, and Python implementations during migration and across targets, but they must be governed by versioned contracts, shared fixtures, differential tests, and promotion gates so search, cache, encoding, self-play, and runtime behavior cannot silently drift.

frontmatter.agent_summary: Tiny Leela may need Rust, TypeScript, and Python implementations during migration and across targets, but they must be governed by versioned contracts, shared fixtures, differential tests, and promotion gates so search, cache, encoding, self-play, and runtime behavior cannot silently drift.
depends_on: [[Design - Runtime target matrix and workflow delegation]]

agent_summary: Inference performance is a first-class roadmap lane, not a cleanup task: Tiny Leela should repeatedly benchmark, optimize, quantize, cache, batch, and parity-check every deployable model/backend across browser, Node, native CPU, Mac mini, and GPU-adjacent workflows.

frontmatter.agent_summary: Inference performance is a first-class roadmap lane, not a cleanup task: Tiny Leela should repeatedly benchmark, optimize, quantize, cache, batch, and parity-check every deployable model/backend across browser, Node, native CPU, Mac mini, and GPU-adjacent workflows.
related: [[Design - Runtime target matrix and workflow delegation]]

links: Design - Runtime target matrix and workflow delegation
title: Design - Inference optimization

agent_summary: Tiny Leela should treat ONNX as a portable model artifact, not as one universal runtime. Browser WebGPU, browser WASM fallback, local CUDA, M-chip Mac mini, and AWS Batch workers need different execution providers, while Rust, TypeScript, and Python should be delegated by boundary: Rust for chess/search/cache hot paths, TypeScript for UI/browser glue, and Python/PyTorch for learning/export/orchestration.

frontmatter.agent_summary: Tiny Leela should treat ONNX as a portable model artifact, not as one universal runtime. Browser WebGPU, browser WASM fallback, local CUDA, M-chip Mac mini, and AWS Batch workers need different execution providers, while Rust, TypeScript, and Python should be delegated by boundary: Rust for chess/search/cache hot paths, TypeScript for UI/browser glue, and Python/PyTorch for learning/export/orchestration.
frontmatter.title: Design - Runtime target matrix and workflow delegation

links: Design - Cross-language contracts and drift control
title: Design - Runtime target matrix and workflow delegation
